daily_brief: The NATO summit in Ankara was overshadowed by escalating US-Iran hostilities, with President Trump declaring the ceasefire 'over' after Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz [BBC World, France 24, Globe and Mail]. US forces struck approximately 90 Iranian military targets, prompting Tehran to retaliate against US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar [France 24, The Hindu]. At the summit, Trump lashed out at allies, calling Spain a 'terrible partner' and renewing his push to acquire Greenland from Denmark, while NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte downplayed tensions as a 'family argument' [BBC Europe, RFI]. In a significant development for Ukraine, Trump offered Kyiv a license to produce Patriot missiles domestically [BBC Europe]. South Korea's Supreme Court upheld former president Yoon Suk Yeol's seven-year prison sentence for crimes related to his 2024 martial law declaration [France 24]. Meanwhile, the EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service confirmed Western Europe experienced its hottest June on record, with temperatures more than 3°C above the 1991-2020 average [RFI, France 24, Le Monde]. Tropical Storm Maysam has killed at least 39 people in southern China's Guangxi region, with Typhoon Bavi now threatening Taiwan and eastern China [Al Jazeera, The Hindu].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Exchange Strikes as Trump Declares Ceasefire 'Over'
bullets: US forces struck approximately 90 Iranian military targets along Iran's southern coastline, including port facilities in Sirik and Bandar Abbas on the Strait of Hormuz
Iran's Revolutionary Guard retaliated by targeting US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar, with Kuwait actively intercepting incoming drones and missiles

Trump declared the fragile ceasefire 'over' following Iranian attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, but later said he expected the flare-up to end quickly and left the door open for more talks
Oil prices surged approximately 8% on Wednesday with Brent topping $80 per barrel before stabilizing Thursday, while Washington revoked a temporary sanctions waiver for Iranian oil

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on military operational details and Trump's statements

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized oil market impacts and economic implications

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the threat to the interim peace deal

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided regional perspective on Gulf state involvement

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on economic dimensions including China factory prices

top_stories.headline: Trump Attacks NATO Allies Over Spain, Greenland at Ankara Summit
bullets: Trump called Spain a 'terrible partner in NATO' and a 'wasted cause,' instructing Treasury Secretary Bessent to cut off trade relations over Madrid's refusal to allow US bases for Iran operations
Trump reiterated his desire for US control of Greenland, calling European resistance 'a big problem' and stating the territory is 'very important for the United States'
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte downplayed the tensions, comparing them to a 'family argument' and insisting Trump is 'completely committed to NATO'
Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez said relations with the US were 'very positive' after an informal chat with Trump about the World Cup

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage of Trump's statements and European responses

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the Iran context driving Trump's frustrations

sources.name: The Economist
sources.framing_note: Focused on Rutte's diplomatic flattery strategy to manage Trump

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Highlighted European uncertainty over Greenland messaging

top_stories.headline: Western Europe Records Hottest June on Record Amid Ongoing Heatwave
bullets: Average temperature in Western Europe reached 20.74°C in June, more than 3°C above the 1991-2020 norm, according to the EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service
The record broke the previous June high set in 2025, when temperatures were 2.81°C above average
A 'heat dome' high-pressure system led to all-time temperature records in multiple countries, with France recording its highest-ever average temperatures on June 24-25
Europe is identified as the world's fastest-warming continent, with a third heatwave since May currently battering the region

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed temperature data and scientific context

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the ongoing nature of heat extremes

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article available but content could not be fully accessed

top_stories.headline: South Korea's Supreme Court Upholds Seven-Year Sentence for Former President Yoon
bullets: South Korea's highest court upheld ex-president Yoon Suk Yeol's seven-year prison sentence for crimes tied to his botched December 2024 martial law declaration

Charges included obstructing cabinet deliberations by convening only select ministers, creating and destroying a forged martial law decree with fake prime minister signatures, and using security agents to block his arrest
Yoon is already in detention while appealing a separate life sentence for leading an insurrection with his martial law declaration
The disgraced former president maintains his martial law declaration was motivated by the public interest

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage of the legal details and charges

top_stories.headline: Trump Offers Ukraine License to Produce Patriot Missiles Domestically
bullets: President Trump told Ukraine's Zelensky at the NATO summit that the US would provide a license for Ukraine to manufacture Patriot interceptor missiles
Trump acknowledged he had not yet informed defense manufacturers Lockheed Martin and Raytheon of his decision
The US used more than half of its Patriot stockpile during its war with Iran earlier this year, limiting its ability to provide missiles directly
Ukraine formally requested US authorization for licensed Patriot production in late May amid increased Russian ballistic missile strikes causing dozens of deaths in Kyiv

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of production challenges and US stockpile limitations
